reviewNotes: A and x are alternative editorial streams of one work and must never be summed as two works; title matter and apparatus are representation layers excluded from work extent.
Complete IIIF bundles preserve whole codices. Their work_portion locators identify Bevis surfaces without representing the cached ZIPs as cropped work-only downloads.

The Internet Archive raw U-M package is an aggregate wrapper containing two distinct layers: raw XML encoding and 516-TIFF facsimile. The same complete ZIP is intentionally projected on both package-download and TIFF-facsimile access.
The normalized copied_from assertion is limited to the exact transformations and canonical equality recorded in evidence:xml-normalization-audit; it does not claim byte identity.

Rights remain component-, jurisdiction-, access-, and provider-layer specific; medieval wording, historical edition text, modern editorial content, scans, XML, metadata, and package wrapper do not inherit one another's status.

The misleading Internet Archive item romanceofsirbeue00kluoft resolves to The Four Sons of Aymon rather than Bevis; its metadata and audited payload are retained as rejected-source evidence, not lineage ancestors.

lineage.summary: The General Corpus artifact is the pinned anz2316 XML, copied from the raw University of Michigan CME encoding after fully classified identifier, editorial-declaration, and whitespace changes. The U-M source package separately contains the raw XML and a complete 516-TIFF facsimile of Kölbing's complete 1885-1894 three-part edition. Kölbing presents one anonymous Middle English Bevis of Hampton through two alternative, non-additive editorial streams: A, based on Auchinleck with 176 missing lines supplied from Egerton S and broad collation; and x, based on Chetham M and Pynson O with de Worde L/O variants. Complete-codex NLS and Trinity IIIF bundles, parallel historical and modern editions, manuscript/print access, layer-specific rights, rejected source controls, and all unresolved locator, chronology, source-redaction, and licensing conflicts remain distinct.

formStatements.description: The whole intellectual work is verse. The Database of Middle English Romance describes lines 1-474 chiefly as six-line tail-rhyme stanzas (aa4b2cc4b2) and lines 475-end as rhyming couplets (aa4bb4, etc.); this is a qualified work-level metrical account, not an additive extent claim. Editorial title matter and apparatus do not change the intellectual work's verse form.

A and x are alternative streams and must never be summed as two works.
The XML x label is an 1886 editorial-stream label, not the later 1894 stemmatic branch v and not a witness siglum.
A metrics exclude all NOTE1 descendants; x metrics additionally exclude the nonverse display L '2071 ff.:'.
Carl Schmirgel's appendix is omitted and therefore does not create a work-level metadata responsibility.
Only the parent content part has item_count=1; all representation children have item_count=null.
Part II's title is imprinted 1886 while its preliminary forewords are dated October 1887; the chronology remains explicit rather than normalized away.

D-MER's work-level form account places chiefly six-line tail-rhyme stanzas in lines 1-474 and rhyming couplets from line 475 to the end; retain that transition as qualified form description only, without adding representation line totals. The metadata lineage-binding schema uses coarse roles: source_package and page_image_carrier map to related_source; transcription_base, missing_leaf_supply, collated_witness, selected_variant_source, transcription_base_and_collated_source, collated_fragment, and supporting_witness map to source_witness; parallel_scholarly_edition and abstract_source_work map to related_source. summaries.value: An anonymous Middle English verse romance in which Bevis is dispossessed and exiled, serves and fights abroad, marries Josian, returns to recover his inheritance, and founds a dynasty through adventures involving warfare, conversion, family separation, and reunion.

The XML represents Kölbing's A stream, with 176 missing-A lines supplied from S, and his 1886-labeled lower x stream based on M and O with L/O variants. These are alternative editorial streams rather than additive works.
